title: notodanthonia occidentalis
intro: Notodanthonia occidentalis, also known as Western danthonia grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that is native to Western Australia, where it can be found growing in dry to moist soils. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The leaves and stems of Notodanthonia occidentalis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
